component.name: Accordion
component.description: A titled, collapsible section. The header row shows a title (with an optional leading icon) and a chevron that toggles between expanded (chevron-up) and collapsed (chevron-down); the content slot below is revealed when expanded. A divider is rendered at the end to separate it from the next section. Modelled on the 'Advanced Settings' disclosure in the article-create form.
component.note: Built from a product screenshot — no dedicated Figma node yet, so there is no .figma.tsx code-connect mapping. Controlled (isExpanded + onToggle) or uncontrolled (defaultExpanded). Follows the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern: a <button> with aria-expanded / aria-controls wrapped in a heading, controlling a labelled region. Sharp corners; the trailing divider uses neutral.200.

header.element: a <button> wrapped in an h{headingLevel} (default h3)
header.layout: full-width flex row, space-between, gap spacing.md (16px)
header.padding: spacing.md (16px) top and bottom; flush horizontally to the container
header.title: Inter bold 18/28 (font-size.md / line-height.md), colors.text.neutral.strong (--text-neutral-strong, neutral.800)
header.chevron: 20px chevron-up when expanded / chevron-down when collapsed, colors.text.neutral.default (neutral.600); group-hover deepens to neutral.800

region.padding: spacing.md (16px) bottom; the slot owns its own internal layout

separator.role: separator (a thematic break, like an <hr>; exposed to assistive tech)
separator.note: Rendered at the end of the accordion; removable via hideSeparator

useCases: Tuck secondary or advanced settings under a labelled disclosure (e.g. 'Advanced Settings')
Group a long form into collapsible sections so the page stays scannable
Show or hide supplementary detail without navigating away
Stack several together (each with its trailing divider) to form a sectioned list

commonPatterns.name: Advanced settings disclosure
commonPatterns.description: A collapsed-by-default section holding optional settings; expanding reveals the controls.

commonPatterns.name: Open by default
commonPatterns.description: Start expanded when the content is primary rather than supplementary.

commonPatterns.name: Controlled
commonPatterns.description: Drive the open state from your own state and react to onToggle (e.g. single-open accordion groups).

commonPatterns.name: Leading icon
commonPatterns.description: Add a glyph before the title to signal the section's purpose.

commonPatterns.name: Last item, no divider
commonPatterns.description: Drop the trailing divider when the accordion is the final item in a bordered group.

antiPatterns.scenario: Hiding essential, always-needed content behind a collapsed accordion
antiPatterns.reason: Critical information should not require an extra click to discover.
antiPatterns.alternative: Show essential content inline; reserve the accordion for optional or advanced detail (or set defaultExpanded).

antiPatterns.scenario: Using an accordion as a navigation menu
antiPatterns.reason: Accordion expands in-place content; it is not a menu of destinations.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use SideNavigation for navigation, or DropdownMenu / Menu for action lists.

antiPatterns.scenario: Putting a single short line of text in an accordion
antiPatterns.reason: The collapse affordance adds friction with no payoff for trivial content.
The header is the only thing visible when collapsed and is the region's accessible name.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always give the accordion a concise, descriptive title.

slots.title: Header label shown next to the chevron (and the region's accessible name)
slots.icon: Optional leading Icon before the title
slots.children: The collapsible content region — any nodes (fields, cards, prose, nested components)

parentConstraints: Renders full-width; the header is flush to the container's horizontal edges

interactions.toggle: Clicking the header (or pressing Enter/Space on it) flips expanded; the chevron swaps between chevron-up and chevron-down
interactions.hover: The chevron deepens from neutral.600 to neutral.800
interactions.uncontrolled: Manages its own open state from defaultExpanded (default false)
interactions.controlled: When isExpanded is provided the component is controlled; update it from onToggle(nextExpanded)

behavior.controlled: Optional. Pass isExpanded + onToggle to control; otherwise uncontrolled via defaultExpanded.

accessibility.role: The header is a button wrapped in a heading (h{headingLevel}); the content is a region
accessibility.ariaExpanded: aria-expanded on the trigger reflects the open state
accessibility.ariaControls: aria-controls links the trigger to the region's id
accessibility.accessibleName: The region is labelled by the trigger via aria-labelledby; the title is the accessible name
accessibility.headingLevel: headingLevel (default 3) sets the wrapping heading so the document outline stays correct
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab moves focus to the header button; Enter/Space toggles; the collapsed region is removed from the tab order and the a11y tree (native hidden)
accessibility.focusManagement: 2px forest.700 focus-visible ring (inset) on the trigger
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a collapsed/expanded button; the revealed content is exposed as a region named by the title
accessibility.contrastNote: neutral.800 title and body text on white and the forest.700 focus ring meet AA; the neutral.600 chevron is a non-essential decorative affordance.

athenaConstraints.separator: 1px neutral.200 divider at the end, removable via hideSeparator
athenaConstraints.noAnimation: Collapse uses the native hidden attribute (no height animation) for predictable layout and a11y

aiHints.context: Reach for Accordion to tuck optional or advanced content behind a labelled, collapsible header — 'Advanced Settings', grouped form sections, supplementary detail. Pass title + children; add an icon for a section glyph, defaultExpanded to start open, hideSeparator for the last item in a group, and isExpanded + onToggle for controlled / single-open groups.
neverUseWhen: The content is essential and always needed — show it inline (or defaultExpanded)
You need navigation between destinations — use SideNavigation
You need an action/option menu — use Menu or DropdownMenu
The content is a single trivial line — render it inline
The content must block the page for a decision — use Modal
